message: Quick sync at 2pm works for me. I'll bring the updated wireframes.
Thanks for handling the client call yesterday. Really appreciated.
The deployment went smoothly. No issues reported so far.
Could you review my PR when you get a chance? No rush.
I'll be working from home tomorrow. Available on Slack as usual.
Good catch on that bug. I've pushed the fix to staging.
Running slightly behind on the documentation. Will have it by Thursday.
The new intern seems to be settling in well. Good hire.
Quarterly review meeting moved to Friday. Calendar invite sent.
Anyone fancy grabbing lunch? Trying that new place on King Street.
Bit knackered this week. Looking forward to the weekend.
The project scope keeps expanding. Need to flag this in the retro.
Sorry, missed your message earlier. Back online now.
Can we reschedule our 1:1? Something came up.
Not my best presentation but got through it. Room for improvement.
Traffic was a nightmare. Finally at my desk.
The spec is a bit unclear on edge cases. Can we clarify?
Heading out for a dental appointment. Back by 3.
Good progress on sprint goals. Should hit all targets.
Mind if I pick your brain about the architecture later?
Been finding it harder to get going in the mornings lately. Sure it'll pass.
The work's fine but I'm not feeling that spark I used to have for it.
Skipped the team social again. Just didn't have the energy.
Another meeting that could have been an email. Getting harder to stay engaged.
Taking longer to recover from busy weeks than I used to.
Is it just me or does everything feel like wading through treacle lately?
I'll do it but honestly struggling to see why it matters.
Caught myself just staring at my screen for 20 minutes. Couldn't start.
The commute feels longer even though nothing's changed.
Used to volunteer for stretch projects. Can't remember the last time I did.
I'm running on empty. Even weekends don't recharge me anymore.
Starting to resent the ping of notifications. Every message feels like a demand.
I used to care about code quality. Now I just want it to work.
Went through three meetings today on autopilot. Couldn't tell you what was discussed.
The gap between what I'm doing and what I wanted to become keeps growing.
Everyone's problems land on my desk. I've got nothing left to give.
Took a sick day but I wasn't ill. Just couldn't face logging in.
I've stopped suggesting improvements. What's the point? Nothing changes.
My partner says I'm different. More distant. They're probably right.
The thought of another quarter of this makes me feel physically heavy.
I'm completely depleted. There's nothing left. I can't pretend anymore.
I don't care if the project fails. I don't care about anything here.
Every morning is a battle just to get dressed. Work feels impossible.
I've mentally checked out months ago. Just going through motions until something breaks.
My body is screaming at me to stop. Constant headaches, can't sleep, stomach issues.
I used to be good at this. Now I make mistakes constantly. I've lost myself.
Sitting in the car park unable to go in. Third time this week.
I fantasise about getting hit by a car so I don't have to come in. Not to die. Just to stop.
Handed in my notice in my head a hundred times. Only thing stopping me is fear.
I'm not a person anymore. Just a function that executes tasks badly.
Bit nervous about the board presentation. Want to make a good impression.
Lot on this week with the release. Manageable but tight.
Three deadlines overlapping. Going to need to prioritise carefully.
Mind's racing a bit with everything going on. Hard to switch off.
Didn't sleep great last night thinking about the migration.
Getting that Sunday evening dread more often lately.
Small things are irritating me more than they should. Need to watch that.
Appetite's been off. Stress eating or not eating at all depending on the day.
Finding it hard to context-switch between all the priorities.
Keep checking emails outside hours. Can't seem to disconnect.
Haven't slept properly in two weeks. Lying awake running through scenarios.
My hands were shaking in the meeting. Hoping no one noticed.
The to-do list grows faster than I can work. Constantly drowning.
Cancelled on friends again. Can't be present for anyone outside work.
Heart pounds every time a senior person messages me. Even for normal things.
Making stupid mistakes because I can't concentrate. Then stressing about the mistakes.
Eating lunch at my desk again. Fifth day in a row. No time.
Snapped at a colleague today. Apologised but I'm not okay.
Weekend didn't help. Came back Monday feeling like I never left.
Doctor mentioned my blood pressure. Said it's work-related. I'm 29.
Had a full panic attack before the client call. Couldn't breathe. Had to excuse myself.
I can't stop crying. Started in a meeting and couldn't stop. So embarrassed.
Haven't eaten a proper meal in days. The anxiety kills any appetite.
Chest pains sent me to A&E. They said it was stress. Told me to slow down.
I'm at breaking point. One more thing and I will shatter completely.
Hands trembling so badly I can barely type. Been like this all week.
Called in sick but I'm not physically ill. I just can't. My body won't let me.
Having intrusive thoughts about everything going wrong. Can't make them stop.
My therapist is concerned. Talking about signed off work. Maybe they're right.
Vomited from stress before the presentation. This isn't normal. This isn't sustainable.
Noticed I'm rarely included in the informal catch-ups anymore.
Third time my contribution wasn't credited in the summary. Coincidence?
My manager's feedback feels different to what others get. More critical somehow.
The jokes about my background are getting more frequent. 'Just banter' apparently.
Keep getting interrupted when I present. Others speak uninterrupted.
Asked to take notes again. Always me. Never the other analysts.
Comments about my clothing choices are becoming regular. Makes me uncomfortable.
Information seems to reach me last. Meetings scheduled when I'm unavailable.
My flexible working requests get scrutinised more than others'.
The nickname they use for me has an edge to it. Hard to explain.
My ideas get dismissed then praised when a male colleague repeats them.
I've been excluded from three client dinners now. Always 'limited space'.
The comments about my pregnancy affecting my commitment are documented now.
My accent gets mocked in team settings. When I object I'm 'too sensitive'.
Work that should come to me goes to less experienced colleagues. Consistently.
I've been moved desks four times. Always further from the team.
Screenshots saved of the comments in the group chat. HR will need evidence.
Performance review was harsh compared to peers with similar output. I checked.
The 'jokes' have shifted to my personal life. Partners, relationships. Inappropriate.
Put on a PIP despite meeting all targets. Others miss targets with no consequence.
Filed my third HR complaint. Nothing changes. Starting to think nothing will.
I've recorded conversations now. The things said when they think no one's listening.
Signed off with anxiety. Doctor says it's a hostile work environment reaction.
Constructive dismissal. That's what my solicitor called it. Forcing me out.
I'm not the first to leave citing this manager. I've spoken to others.
The retaliation after my complaint has been swift. Stripped of key responsibilities.
I dread seeing certain colleagues. Physically sick walking into the office.
My mental health has deteriorated significantly. Direct result of the treatment here.
Seeking legal advice on discrimination. Have eighteen months of documented incidents.
I've lost trust in leadership entirely. They've protected the perpetrator at every turn.
Feeling a bit flat this week. Nothing specific, just low energy.
Haven't been enjoying things I usually do. Film club felt like a chore.
Sleep's been off. Either can't get to sleep or can't get up.
Been keeping to myself more. Don't really feel like socialising.
Finding it hard to get started on tasks. Everything feels effortful.
Appetite's not great. Food doesn't appeal like it used to.
Concentration is patchy. Reading the same paragraph multiple times.
Bit tearful lately. Not sure why. Just feels close to the surface.
The weekend felt like something to get through rather than enjoy.
Motivation is low. Doing the minimum to get by at the moment.
Can't remember the last time I genuinely laughed. Everything feels grey.
Forcing myself through each day. It's exhausting just existing.
Sleep 10 hours and still wake up exhausted. Something's not right.
Made a significant error on the project. Mind isn't working properly.
Cancelled plans again. The thought of engaging with people is overwhelming.
I look at my past work and wonder who that person was. Not me anymore.
Started crying at my desk. Had to pretend it was hayfever.
The simplest decisions feel impossible. What to eat. What to wear. Paralysed.
Colleagues have noticed I'm different. I can see the concern. Can't explain.
Feel like I'm watching my life through a window. Not really present.
I've become a burden to the team. They'd function better without me.
Can't see things improving. This is just how it is now. How I am.
Haven't showered in days. Can't find the point in basic self-care.
The world would be simpler without me in it. Not saying I'd do anything.
I'm broken in a way that can't be fixed. Fundamentally defective.
Everything is dark and heavy. Getting through each hour is a victory.
I've thought about how I'd do it. Not planning. Just thinking.
My doctor has referred me to crisis services. Things have escalated.
I don't want to die but I don't want to live like this either.
Writing this is hard. Everything is hard. Existence is exhausting.
Been researching methods. Just information gathering. Need to understand options.
Found myself wondering if anyone would miss me. Probably not for long.
The thought of not existing brings relief. First time I've felt peace.
Life insurance policy would take care of everything. At least there's that.
Sometimes driving home I think about not turning the wheel. Just once.
I've been giving away some of my things. Don't need them anymore.
Written letters to people. Just in case. Feels responsible.
I know exactly how I'd do it. Have done for a while now. It's comforting.
Cancelled long-term appointments. Won't be needing them.
The date feels right. Something to work towards finally.
Tried last night. Failed obviously. Not sure if relieved or disappointed.
I've acquired what I need. It's in my flat. Ready when I am.
Tonight feels right. Wanted to say this team was the best part.
Everything is in order. Passwords documented. Handover notes done. Ready.
This is goodbye. I'm sorry I couldn't be stronger. Don't blame yourselves.
Can you send the stakeholder list? Need it for the comms plan.
Jenkins build is green. Ready for QA.
Booked meeting room 4B for the design review.
Nice work on the refactor. Much cleaner now.
OOO next Tuesday for a training course.
The retrospective raised good points. Let's action them.
Happy to help onboard the new starter.
Pushed the fix. Should resolve the timeout issues.
Let's grab coffee and discuss the roadmap.
Thanks for covering while I was away.
Starting to feel like I'm running to stand still.
My patience for meetings about meetings is running out.
Dreaming about work. Can't escape it even in sleep.
The microaggressions are relentless. Death by a thousand cuts.
Getting through today is the only goal I can manage.
Everything I built here is being dismantled. My legacy erased.
The walls are closing in. Every day tighter.
I see now why people burn out. I thought I was different. I wasn't.
My mind keeps going to dark places. Trying to redirect but it's hard.
I'm not okay but I don't know how to ask for help.
Team drinks after work if anyone's interested.
PR approved. Merging to main.
Client loved the demo. Great team effort.
Adjusting my hours slightly next week for childcare.
The documentation looks comprehensive. Well done.
New joiner starting Monday. Please make them welcome.
Grabbed the meeting action items. Will circulate.
Infrastructure costs came in under budget this quarter.
Anyone have experience with the new testing framework?
Really enjoyed collaborating on this sprint.
My body keeps score even when my mind denies it. Constantly ill now.
I've become someone I don't recognise. Short-tempered. Cynical. Withdrawn.
They've created a paper trail to justify forcing me out. I see it clearly.
Woke up disappointed to be awake. That's new.
Everything feels performative now. Acting human in meetings.
The physical symptoms are mounting. Tinnitus. Vertigo. Hives.
I've alienated everyone who cared. The isolation is complete.
My desk feels like a prison. The hours crawl.
I used to mentor others. Now I can barely help myself.
Checking the clock constantly. Willing time to pass.
Overheard them laughing about me. Not even pretending to hide it now.
The panic comes in waves. Sometimes at rest. No trigger needed.
I've stopped making future plans. What's the point.
Every criticism confirms what I already know. I'm worthless.
This will be my last week. One way or another.
Sprint planning at 10. Bring your estimates.
Merged the feature branch. Deployment scheduled for tonight.
Thanks for the thorough code review. Addressed all comments.
Working through the backlog today. Good progress.
The API documentation is now live on Confluence.
Lunch order going in at 12. Let me know if you want anything.
I'll handle the support tickets this afternoon.
Great standup today. Clear priorities.
Heads up: server maintenance window 2-4am tonight.
The quarterly figures look solid. Well done team.
Flight booked for the conference next month.
New Figma designs uploaded. Feedback welcome.
Picked up the accessibility ticket. Starting tomorrow.
Good pairing session today. Learned a lot.
The cache invalidation issue is resolved.
Coffee run. The usual for everyone?
Updated the wiki with the new process.
Client feedback was positive. They're happy to proceed.
Grabbing 30 mins for a walk. Back soon.
End of day. Picking up tomorrow morning.
I've been carrying a weight I can't name. It's getting heavier.
Remember when this job excited me? Feels like another life.
The thought of another year here makes me want to cry.
I've stopped caring about my career progression. What's the point.
Everyone wants a piece of me and there's nothing left to give.
I'm professionally hollow. Going through the motions.
The only thing getting me through is counting days to holidays.
Used to be first in, last out. Now I resent every extra minute.
I've forgotten what it feels like to be proud of my work.
My manager asked if I'm okay. I lied and said yes.
The Sunday scaries have become the permanent scaries.
My jaw hurts from clenching. Dentist says it's stress.
I've developed a twitch. Appeared last month. Won't stop.
Four energy drinks to get through today. Not sustainable.
Every email could be bad news. I hover before opening.
My hair is falling out. Clumps in the shower. Stress apparently.
Blood pressure medication at 32. Thanks work.
The ringing in my ears is constant now. Tinnitus from stress.
I've aged visibly in six months. People comment on it.
Stress leave is looking increasingly necessary.
They questioned my competence in front of the client.
My religion was mocked at the team lunch. Again.
Being called 'aggressive' for the same assertiveness praised in others.
The inappropriate comments about my body are documented.
My disability accommodations are treated as special favours.
They deadname me deliberately. HR says it's 'adjustment period'.
Excluded from the promotion pool. No explanation that makes sense.
The bullying has escalated since I complained. Classic retaliation.
My workspace keeps being vandalised. Small things. Deniable.
I've been told to 'go back where I came from'. By a manager.
Colours feel muted. Like someone turned down the saturation on life.
I used to have opinions. Now I just agree with whatever.
Three weeks since I've seen friends. Don't have the energy to pretend.
I've stopped opening personal messages. Can't face them.
The emptiness is so loud. It's all I can feel.
I've written and deleted this message ten times. Everything is hard.
My flat is a mess. Can't bring myself to clean. Weeks now.
People keep asking if I'm okay. I must look as bad as I feel.
Went to the GP. They've referred me for assessment.
The medication isn't working. Might need to try something else.
Thinking about what happens when I'm gone. Not if. When.
The bridge on my commute. I notice it more now.
Looked up statistics on survival rates. Just curious.
My pets are the only reason. Without them I don't know.
The void is calling louder than anything else.
Sorted my digital accounts. Passwords documented for family.
Been saying goodbye without saying goodbye. People don't notice.
The calm I feel now is strange. Like the decision is made.
Called the helpline last night. They talked me down. This time.
I've written the note. Revised it. It's ready.
Project kickoff went smoothly. Good energy in the room.
Tests passing. Coverage at 87%.
Thanks for staying late to help with the release.
The new hire is ramping up quickly. Good addition.
Retro scheduled for Friday 3pm.
I've lost the ability to feel joy. Or sadness. Just nothing.
Every day I wake up disappointed that I did.
The effort of existing is too much. Everything is too much.
I'm a ghost already. Just waiting for the rest to catch up.
Therapy appointment today. Dreading it and needing it equally.
I zone out for hours now. Time just disappears.
The cynicism has infected everything. Can't see good in anything.
I've been drinking more. Helps me switch off from work.
My relationships are suffering. I have nothing left for anyone.
Compassion fatigue they call it. I'm just empty.
New starter orientation on Thursday. Happy to help.
Pipeline deployment automated. Saves hours weekly.
Team dinner booked for the 15th. Menu options in thread.
Solid quarter. Rest up this weekend everyone.
The monitoring alerts are tuned. Should reduce noise.
Four panic attacks this week. A new record.
Can't distinguish between real threats and my anxiety anymore.
My body is in constant fight-or-flight. Even at rest.
The intrusive thoughts are relentless. Work catastrophes on loop.
Considering inpatient treatment. Things are that bad.
Prescribed beta blockers for the physical symptoms.
Mindfulness doesn't touch this anymore. It's too deep.
The constant vigilance is exhausting. I can't relax.
Waking at 3am with racing thoughts. Every night now.
My nervous system is fried. I can feel it.
The intrusive thoughts about ending it come unbidden now.
I've calculated how long before anyone would notice I'm gone.
Death feels like the only certainty. Strangely comforting.
My search history would worry anyone who saw it.
The world keeps spinning without needing me. I've tested this.
I've stopped taking precautions. Reckless in small ways.
Giving away meaningful items. People think I'm decluttering.
The exit feels closer now. Less abstract. More real.
Made amends with people I've wronged. Tying up loose ends.
My will is updated. Just being responsible. That's all.
The pain of staying exceeds the fear of leaving.
Tonight. I've decided. This message is my note.
I've attempted before. They don't know. I know how it feels now.
The calm is absolute. The decision brings peace.
If you're reading this I didn't make it to Monday.
PR looks good. Approved and merged.
Standup at 9:15 instead of 9 tomorrow.
Infrastructure costs down 12% this month.
The load balancer config is optimised.
Lunch at the usual place? Noon?
Leave approved for next Friday.
The monitoring dashboard is live.
Solid demo. Client is proceeding to pilot.
Expenses submitted for the trip.
New team member starting next sprint.
They mimicked my accent in the meeting. Everyone laughed.
My workspace was moved without notice or explanation.
Consistently assigned the worst on-call shifts.
My emails get shorter responses than others'. Curt. Dismissive.
The comments about my age are becoming more frequent.
They've started excluding me from code reviews I should be on.
HR suggested I was 'misinterpreting' documented incidents.
My development budget was cut. No one else's was.
Being set up to fail with impossible deadlines. Only me.
The hostile environment claim is being prepared by my solicitor.
Witnesses have come forward. I'm not the only target.
Constructive dismissal case being built. Documented everything.
The psychological damage will take years to undo.
I don't feel safe in this workplace anymore.
Resigned effective immediately. Citing hostile environment.
Bit overwhelmed but handling it. Usual project stress.
More coffee than water today. Need the energy.
Tension headaches most days now. Paracetamol helps.
The notification sound triggers a small stress response now.
Finding it harder to leave work at work mentally.
The knot in my stomach is permanent now.
Stress is affecting my memory. Forgetting important things.
Irritable bowel flare-ups coinciding with work pressure.
The muscle tension is constant. Physio says it's stress-held.
My resting heart rate is elevated. Fitbit confirms it.
Numbness down my arm scared me. Stress apparently.
The future feels like a blank wall. Nothing there.
Everyone seems to be living while I'm just existing.
My hobbies feel like chores now. All of them.
Time moves differently when you're barely present.
The heaviness is physical. Like gravity increased for me alone.
I rehearse being okay. It's exhausting.
Comfort eating or not eating. No middle ground.
The darkness has edges now. I can feel its shape.
Crying for no reason. Started on the tube. Couldn't stop.
Sleep is the only relief. I want more of it.
I've stopped making plans. There's no point.
The void inside me has won.
I'm tired in a way sleep doesn't fix.
Nothing helps. Nothing changes. Nothing matters.
My capacity for caring is completely depleted.
Work follows me into dreams. Nightmares really.
I used to solve problems. Now I just survive them.
The enthusiasm died quietly. Didn't notice until it was gone.
Every interaction feels transactional now. Nothing genuine.
I've forgotten why I chose this career.
They say it's a marathon not a sprint. I stopped running.
My body is forcing me to stop. Multiple health issues.
The cynicism is a defence mechanism. I know this.
Autopilot is my default state now.
Deployment completed successfully.
Thanks for covering my tickets yesterday.
Updated the roadmap with Q2 priorities.
Interview panel confirmed for Thursday.
The new CI pipeline is much faster.
Annual leave balance: 18 days remaining.
Security training completed.
Good collaboration on the incident response.
Feedback from the retro implemented.
The architecture review went well.
Docs updated with the new API endpoints.
Volunteering for the hackathon committee.
Test coverage improved to 91%.
Picked up the security ticket. Starting now.
Good progress on the migration plan.
Weekend plans: completely nothing. Perfect.
The refactor improved performance by 40%.
Thanks for the mentoring session. Really helpful.
Heading to the conference room for the demo.
The PR template is really helping quality.
I exist in a state of constant emergency now.
My doctor wants me off work. I'm resisting but maybe they're right.
Functioning on adrenaline and caffeine. Nothing else.
The weight of it all is crushing me physically.
I'm one bad day from complete breakdown.
